sheet.bio:
Overview

Name: Valkera Blackheart (Née Goldenthal) 

Race: Human 

Gender: Female 

Spouse: Thorin Blackheart 

Age: 36 

Culture: Thaian 

Birth: Unknown

Height: 1,72m 

Personality: Diligent, arrogant, irritable

Defining Traits

Feeble Frame

This character was born delicate and is often sick and bruises easily.

Blackheart In-law

This character married into the House of Blackheart.

Exalted Devotion

This character has dedicated their life to the service of a higher power.

Background

Born among the gutters of Thais, Valkera lived as a thief and pickpocket. Even then, magical healing powers stirred within her, powers she learned to channel into rune tablets. For a time she earned a living with help from the trader Sarra Zoldan, carving healing runes for the Followers of the White Dragon in Cormaya, until exploitation forced her to flee by to Liberty Bay.

There, things were no easier. Her business was ruined, her charity shunned with suspicion and fear. After tending to sick children rejected by the corrupt church, Valkera was branded a cultist and driven from the city.

Exile led her to the Mountain Hideout and the Circle of Golgari. Among mystics and scholars, she found stability in her life. The sorcerer Pertwee taught her to read and write, while the Circle helped her unlock her healing powers. Yet peace did not last.

Drawn into the Dark Side civil war through her companion Equoez, Valkera spent years as a battle mage in the Bey faction, witnessing cruelty, death, and the slow erosion of her ideals. Disillusioned, she deserted and vanished into the Fields of Glory until eventually resurfacing on the island of Senja. There she lived quietly, selling fish to the local population until fate drew her back to the main land.

Valkera met the elf Othelen, and together they travelled to Fibula, where she joined the Red Rose. There, she gained a formal education in stewardship and established a successful trading house in Liberty Bay. She also married into the noble House of Blackheart, and in time, she rose to the Council of Red Rose as Magistra Noviciorum.

But peace and prosperity did not last. When both Othelen and Equoez disappeared, Valkera uncovered a conspiracy aimed at destroying the Red Rose from within. The plot was stopped, but her friends were lost. Othelen was mind-controlled by the ancient Paladin of Darkness known as Osamiron, and ordered to slay Equoez. When the Roses found Othelen, they were forced to slay him too.

Haunted by failure and driven by hatred, Valkera abandoned trade and politics alike. Together with her companion Roderic Rivershaw, she reclaimed the abandoned Mountain Hideout and unearthed its ancient and powerful secrets. From these foundations they forged the Order of the Argent Gryphon, devoted to wisdom, strength, and the destruction of evil.

Under Valkera's leadership, the Argent Gryphon rapidly grew in influence. Alliances were forged, initiates recruited, and old cults hunted beneath the mountains of Vandura. Yet the deeper the Order delved into buried secrets, the darker the discoveries became.

Then came disaster. While Valkera negotiated a defensive pact with Fibula, Roderic fell by a terrible curse. Though still alive, his mind had become distant and unreachable. Valkera halted the Order's campaigns entirely, abandoning plans for war in favour of a desperate search for a cure.

Now, as Lady Warden of the Argent Gryphon, Valkera stands at a crossroads. What are honour, duty, and justice worth beside the warmth of friendship and brotherhood? And how far will she descend to save those she loves?

Overview

Name: Elara Blackthorn
Race: Human
Gender: Female
Age: 29

Place of Birth: Edron


Appearance

Elara appears tall and slender, her straight jet-black hair falls long, right below the waist line. Thin face with pronounced cheekbones, sunken black coloured eyes and wild brows. She prefers to wear long garnet satin dresses and low heels. A small burn scar mark on the left side of her neck.

Personality traits

Quiet at first, Elara tends to listen and then speak with friendly tone. She appreciates creativity and art. She follows her own moral compass and is not afraid to bend rules to achieve what she desires.

Background

"Always remember the light of Banor, my daughter. Even when the night seem endless." 
- Faloria Blackthorn, at Cormaya's tunnels

Elara grew up in the busy streets of Edron, daughter of two devoted servants of Daniel Steelsoul. They carried hope into Cormaya, with the task to stop the Curse that deformed citizens into  werebeasts. It crawled through flesh like cold iron, turning once known friendly faces into blood-hungry monsters. Her parents managed to contain it at Cormaya's dungeons, temporaly holding the darkness at bay. But they never returned to hold her again. In the silence left behind, she felt anger and hatred towards those who sent her parents away. 

She was taken by her grandfather Alexander, the local rune craftsman of Edron. At his workshop, between tall piles of carved Daramian glyphs and grey blank runes, she learned to listen for the flow of mana inside every living creature. Every day she sat beside old Alexander, on a comfortable pillow, as he infused oak snake-shaped rods with strands of energy so fine that reminded her of water dancing down through a creek. From that moment she felt in her heart a great hunger to master that art herself. And he taught her everything he knew. 

“Mana listens to the intent behind the hand that wields it.”
- Alexander

When she was old enough, Elara began adventuring into Edron's old forest when the sun was down, carefully planning routes and tracing paths, looking between moss-covered trunks in search of the perfect branch. She learned how to channel mana into every little form of organic plant matter. Merchants and collectors travelled from Thais, Carlin and even distant Svargrond to place their orders. Her wands were carved with perfection for the needs of healers and summoners, each piece bearing her touch and adding to her reputation. Yet, with every praise and gold coin, a new void opened inside her heart. There was no cure for the emptiness she carried. She wanted something more. She wanted to be feared. 

That emptiness found a voice when she first met with Raelthar. He moved through the shadows at the tavern like a question unanswered, speaking of sorceries that one could only dream of. He offered Elara more than fame or coin: he promised that her name would be spoken in thunder, that her magic could grow strong enough to make those who caused her pain to pay the ultimate price. When she left Edron beside him, her silhoute alligned against the rising moon, and she felt the sentiment of terrible purpose.

Roderic Rivershaw was born in the quiet hamlet of Rosebud, where he grew up among the farmers and fishers. He grew lanky and strong hauling carts and chopping timber for the local blacksmith, his sinewy frame tempered by honest labour. A jagged scar arcs across his left cheek – an echo of a childhood brawl when he defended his master's son from a local bully. His hair is dark as raven’s wing, cropped close except for a single plait down the nape, braided in remembrance of his late mentor. His grey‐steel eyes burn with quiet determination, and when he moves, there’s the steady rhythm of someone who’s spent years aligning body and purpose.

When the trumpets of the Order of the Argent Gryphon sounded through Rosebud, Roderic felt a stirring in his bones. Though he wears the Order’s gleaming breastplate now – an argent gryphon alighting on a field of red – he never forgets the simple leather jerkin beneath it. He greets every noble and peasant alike with the same courtesy, believing that honour is not a gift of birth but a choice of the heart.

Physical Traits

Towering build
Just shy of six foot four, muscular without ever seeming overburdened
Notable features
The scar along his left cheek; a single braided lock of hair with a carved wooden bead
Eyes
Steely grey, level and thoughtful, quick to soften when faced with suffering

Character Traits

Resolute
Once he commits, nothing – neither fear nor fatigue – can sway his will
Compassionate
Instinctively draws to those in need, believing true strength is measured by what one protects
Humble
Wears his knightly mantle lightly, always mindful of the humble hearth where he first learned swordcraft

Character Hooks

Family Legacy
Rumours whisper that his bloodline hides a dormant nobility - will he pursue his ancient heritage or forsake it for his newfound future in the Order
Inner Conflict
Haunted by the moment he drew blood in defence of his cousin, Roderic struggles with the morality of violence even as he stands sworn to martial service.
Old Oath
Before joining the Argent Gryphon, Roderic swore a blood-oath to a dying blacksmith whose life he couldn’t save. Now, the memory of that promise drives him to protect the weak – he fears that one day he may be called upon to fulfill the oath in a way that conflicts with his duty as a knight.

Valarya is a human knight of the Argent Gryphon bearing the rank of Oathsworn, though whispers place his name among the remnants of the ancient Order of the Black Knights — an order long buried beneath myth, denial, and deliberate forgetting.

He is not a man who appears cruel at first meeting. Valarya carries himself with restraint uncommon among knights. He speaks little unless spoken to directly, listens with unsettling attentiveness, and rarely wastes emotion on displays of anger, pride, or zeal. Most remember him first as calm. Reasonable. Measured. The sort of man capable of standing unmoved while others lose themselves to fear or outrage.

That stillness is what draws people toward him. He possesses a talent for understanding what others need to hear, particularly in moments of uncertainty. To the doubtful, he offers certainty. To the ashamed, absolution. To the ambitious, purpose. He does not manipulate through charm so much as through permission — an ability to make people feel their worst instincts are not weaknesses, but necessary truths they were too frightened to admit aloud.

He believes deeply that every institution eventually rots beneath the weight of its own ideals. Honour becomes vanity. Faith becomes control. Mercy becomes weakness exploited by the merciless. Where others react to this decay with outrage or despair, Valarya accepts it with near religious calm. In his view, corruption is not a failure of mankind, but its most honest state.

Despite this, he is neither reckless nor openly sadistic. Violence is a tool he employs without hesitation, but rarely without purpose. He dislikes spectacle, distrusts fanaticism, and views emotional cruelty as wasteful indulgence. What unsettles others most is not brutality, but the absence of visible hatred behind it. Valarya can condemn a man with the same composure another knight might use to discuss the weather.

Among the Argent Gryphon, his influence is difficult to measure because it rarely appears forceful. Decisions made around him simply tend to drift toward harsher conclusions. Compromises become ultimatums. Necessary evils become accepted practice. Over time, men who once considered themselves virtuous find themselves defending acts they would previously have condemned, often without remembering precisely when their thinking changed.

Whether this is deliberate manipulation or merely the consequence of Valarya's presence is unclear. Even his loyalties remain uncertain. Some believe the vows he carries as Oathsworn bind him utterly to the Argent Gryphon. Others suspect those oaths conceal an older allegiance to the forgotten doctrines of the Black Knights. Valarya himself has never clarified the matter, and those few who pressed him on it rarely found the answers comforting.

He has the unnerving habit of speaking about ruin the same way other men speak about winter — not with excitement, nor fear, but with quiet acceptance of something inevitable.

Overview

Name: Mighty Micu
Race: Micu
Vocation: Druid
Age: 18
Religion: Crunor
Inclination: Goodness
Guild: Argent Gryphon

Background

Life in the mountains is really gentle when you are used to the cold. There is good hunting, natural shelter, and a vast variety of herbs for all kinds of needs. Not because of that, the Micu Tribe lives like wild animals, even though they can be wild between themselves. Micus spend most of their time contemplating and reflecting on existence. These long reflections help them to feel the energy of the elements that surround them, and sometimes even dominate it. The kids play conjuring small blizzards and mud projectiles. When they become adults, they use these tools to hunt.

Sometimes, among the Micu people, stronger kids are born, with a restless heart that distracts their partners and makes the adults feel uncomfortable. According to tradition, their destiny is to become a "Machi," but they can't fulfill this rank before they make a pilgrimage in search of Knowledge. For this, when they reach the majority of age, they must abandon the mountain tribe in the cold islands and travel through the lands beyond the sea. Someday, when they become elders, they have to return with the promise of teaching what they have learned to the rest of the tribe.

A young Micu ventured between the waves, paddling in a small canoe. He sailed at night guided by the moon, and by day he reflected on what he could reach on the horizon. Until one day, the biggest canoe he could ever imagine intercepted him in the middle of the sea.

Several heads peered over the ship's rail. They looked curiously at the young boy and threw him a rope. Micu climbed to the ship's deck, surprised by the size of this canoe. While the passengers looked at him, intrigued by his rustic, raw leather clothes, the boy was amused by the bright colors of the foreigners' outfits.

Captain Greyhound: Welcome on board! I am at your service. Greeted the man dressed in blue. (The young man had never heard those words, but seeing his extended hand, he understood it was a greeting)

Mighty Micu: “Amun Machi Micu,” said the boy in his dialect.

Captain Greyhound: Mighty what? He thought and smiled. He heard once about these peaceful men of the sea that spoke a different language. They shook hands until it felt weird. Eventually, people around them got bored of looking and lost interest, while the Captain guided Micu to the dining room, deciding food is the universal language.

Captain Greyhound: “Very well, Mighty Micu! I am going to give you a job on this ship. Nobody travels for free, and I see you don't carry a single gold piece.”

The Captain talked and talked, and the young boy nodded while he ate. Not much time passed until the signs and expressions became evident. Micu worked hard carrying barrels and boxes, listening with open ears to the new words. Greyhound, satisfied with the boy's diligence, hired him for several months, time that Micu used to learn the language.

One day, he dropped one of the barrels in the ship's hold. With difficulty, he read the label: "Liberty Bay - Valkera Blackheart" and heard a moan inside. Curiosity invaded him, and he softly lifted the barrel's lid.

The eyes of a muscled, bald young boy stared at him deeply. Micu saw his clenched hands and decided to place the lid back on the barrel. He could not believe what he saw! He reflected on what he saw during the day, following his tribe's tradition, wondering why someone would travel like that. He did not find any satisfactory answer, but concluded that the boy in the barrel must have been very hungry.

That day, during dinner, he hid inside his new sailor clothes' pockets (provided by the Captain) some chicken meat, and when night fell, he sneaked into the ship's hold to find the barrel. He knocked twice on the barrel's lid to announce himself before opening the barrel softly. The bald boy was still in the same position, and without words, Micu gave him the roasted chicken meat and a leather wineskin. He closed the barrel in silence and headed to his bed to sleep.

Eventually, the mysterious barrel was unloaded in a city called Liberty Bay.

Several new moons later, Micu thanked the Captain for his hospitality with a clumsy but effective goodbye in the mainland’s language. After a tight hug, left the dock and headed to Carlin's streets.

Captain Greyhound: Take care, Micu! Shouted the Captain, waving his arm.

Micu learned new spells from Padreia, Carlin's Grand Druid. He explored new cities and caves, and learned that violence was the universal language in these lands. This new language seemed even more complicated than the mainland language.

Micu became a great explorer. In one of his expeditions, he went back to Liberty Bay (a city he had only seen from Captain Greyhound’s ship) and travelled through these lands. He found a secret hole in an abandoned warehouse outside the city. Hidden dungeons were his favorite.

After slaying a couple of big tarantulas, he heard evil singing from the interior of the caves. He sneaked in silently through the meandering tunnels, guided by the sounds. The smell of blood and fire filled his nose when he saw a circle of cultists around a pile of human corpses.

Micu had heard before rumors of ritual murders, and felt the urge to end this horrible act. He threw a spell of icy rain over these cultists that sang horrible melodies. Furious, they threw themselves back at the young druid, but he repelled them easily, shooting spells of ice and fire. The cultists fell fast to the ground.

After a quick search of the bodies, he found two particular melody sheets, marked as one and two. Micu wanted to find the melody sheet three, so he dug even deeper into the dungeon. Other cultists, unaware of his presence, fell under the druid’s spell, until he found a second group of them gathered around another pile of corpses.

Micu checked his supplies of runes and mana, but when he was ready to launch the attacks, he found himself surrounded by a huge bonfire. He heard the shouts of a witch conjuring the flames.

Nevertheless, he threw an attack back, blocking the other cultists' spells, covering himself with his spellbook. The young druid directed his spells against the witch, who positioned herself at a good distance. Micu had to step back, resisting the incessant attacks. He managed to kill a couple of cultists, but they were instantly replaced by others.

Micu wanted to take another step back, but he found himself against the wall. He could no longer block all the hits thrown by the outraged cultists. The witch kept conjuring flames that burned the young druid’s skin. He had to escape!

Micu thought he was hallucinating when he heard the sound of thunder bouncing in the caves, and electric sparks meandering against the walls of the dungeon. He could no longer attack his aggressors and limited himself to recovering energy, using his magic to survive.

The cultists stopped the attack, falling fulminated in front of him. Powerful lightning piercing through them ended their lives. The witch no longer shouted, and suddenly, a massive silence filled the cave.

Micu stopped covering his face with the spellbook, looked up and saw a young boy in a martial stance in front of the witch’s dead body. Thin lightning sparked through his arms and coiled in his clenched fists. Micu lifted his head to observe the young warrior’s figure, and recognized those fists.

Overview

Name: Myrenthil
Race: Human
Gender: Male
Age: Unkown
Culture: Primordial
Personality: calm, callous, unsettling

Defining traits

Disciple of the First Night
This character belongs to one of the last surviving remnants of an ancient order dating back to the Age of Chaos.
Voice of Collapse
This character believes destruction, decay, and catastrophe are natural forces necessary for renewal and transformation.
Unnerving Presence
This character has a subtle influence on others, drawing forth hidden resentment, doubt, ambition, and suppressed desires.


Background

Myrenthil is one of the last surviving druids of the Order of the First Night, an ancient sect most believe vanished long ago. Branded heretics created by Zathroth, the Order teaches that existence became flawed when Uman forged the Spiral of Time within the Void. To them, the Void was not emptiness, but infinite possibility untouched by permanence, destiny, or decay.

Unlike most druids who seek harmony between civilization and nature, Myrenthil believes prolonged stability breeds weakness. Kingdoms, forests, and even faiths must eventually collapse so that something stronger may emerge from their remains. He does not delight in suffering, yet neither does he fear destruction. Fire clears dead forests, rot nourishes new life, and fallen empires become fertile ground for transformation.

Myrenthil rarely raises his voice or acts with cruelty. Instead, his influence is quiet and deeply unsettling. In his presence, doubts harden into conviction and buried desires rise to the surface. He does not command others toward chaos; he merely strips away restraint and watches what emerges beneath.

To Myrenthil, the First Night is not evil darkness, but the memory of the Void before existence was chained by time and certainty. In abandoned ruins reclaimed by roots and stone worn smooth by centuries, he sees sacred reminders that nothing created by mortals or gods can resist change forever.

Name: Tiases Blackheart
Race: Human 
Gender: Male
Wife: Mayleea Blackheart
Age: 22 
Culture: Deraisim 
Birth: Unknown
Height: 1,66m 
Personality: persistent, cheerful, helpful

History:

He was born crosseyed in harsh conditions. Abandoned by his father due to his disability, in the forests surrounding Mount Sternum, where he was found by patrolling elven scouts whos helps him and took with them because they belive that no one deserved such a fate.

As he grew older, he helped local shopkeepers in Ab'Dendriel, but he especially loved helping the blacksmith and weapons dealer - Shanar. In gratitude, Shanar regularly took him to the royal family so he could delve into the books, learning the basics of honorable combat and respect for nature and other races.

One day, when almost all the elves had left the fortress, either hunting or patrolling, a sudden attack by wild, bloodthirsty animals occurred. They sowed such chaos that the guards the entrance, unable to cope, blew their horns, calling everyone to battle. Initially, Tiases and the weaker elves were supposed to remain in the underground tunnels, awaiting reinforcements.

However, out of nowhere, the young, crosseyed man felt so much strength in himself that he broke out from the elves guarding him, grabbed a random stick, and rushed into battle. Running to the main gate, he had only one thing in mind: to save the lives of the elves who had saved and raised him!

Unfortunately, while trying to help Faluae, who was fighting in close combat, he tripped over a protruding blueberry bush. However, in the midst of this unfortunate event, he stabbed the Alpha-wolf leading the attack—directly and fatally in his eye, causing it to fall instantly. The other animals surrendered without a fight, seeing the fate that had befallen on their Alpha.

The royal family, seeing his bravery, decided to reward him with a special surprise. For months, Tiases and Othelen traveled the Tibian lands, gathering ingredients for the druids, but unaware of their purpose.

When the next fullmoon phase arrived, he was invited with his friends to an ancient stone circle near Carlin, where it turned out they had offered him an inconspicuous vial to drink. After tasting it, the druids immediately began reciting ancient elven spells, and Tiases, gazing in amazement, realized that his body was changing. He gained muscle, the strength of 100 wild horses, and most importantly, his crosseyed gone forever.

He was accepted as an honorary member of the royal guards when all back to town. His life changed completely—he traveled to Shadowthorn for training, spent time with ladies—who suddenly became more willing to approach him. However, he didn't forget his old friends, whom he gladly visited in his every free time.

At one evening feast, he met Shiriel's beautiful daughter - Mayleea, whom he delighted in with his sense of humor. Despite the general opposition to human-elven marriages, they were the only exception to succeed. Blessed by the king and queen themselves, they settled in one of the undertown-cottages where they had children. His wife tended the household and studied white magic, while Tiases helped with trade expeditions.

On one of these expeditions, he met his real mother - Valkera, who, despite his completely different appearance, sensed her blood within him with the magic she wielded. At this moment she was a great Lady Warden of of the Argent Gryphon. She showed him her fortress and Surroundings and promised him that if he ever forgave her, there would always be a place for him there.

Returning home, he kept thinking about this meeting. They also discussed it at length with his wife – but at the end – they ultimately moving to aid the Order of the Argent Gryphon with sword and magic.

Name: Mayleea Blackheart 
Race: Elf 
Gender: Female
Spouse: Tiases Blackheart
Age: 19
Culture: Cenath 
Birth: Unknown
Height: 1,71m 

Personality: caring, good, fair


History:

She was born far from the old great elven wars in one of the leading castes of Ab'Dendriel. As the daughter of Shiriel, she delved into the secrets of white magic and druidry from a young age. Her father was strict with her, but he knew that she would eventually grow into a strong woman who could command the elements of nature.

In her free time, she delighted in helping others, tending the gardens within the fortress and learning new potion ingredients in the city library. Her father kept a close-eye on her and carefully selected her companions so that she would prioritize her own development over fun.

However, a young man – Tiases Blackheart - she had known him since childhood was wandering around the city. He might have been crosseyed, but he was helpful and cheerful for everyone around. As she strolled, she glanced at him with joy in her heart, yet she knew that if she mentioned this at home - her father would never forgive her.

An unfortunate event, however, helped her – beloved young boy became an honorary member of the elven royal guards and the biggest „hottie” in the whole fortress – after drinking a magical elixir. She knew this was her chance - she took the advantage of during the evening feast - where he made her laugh until she cried, and she fell in love with him.

Thus, she became a member of the noble Blackheart family. The royal couple themselves attended their wedding, offering them their own undertown-cottage in return for her husband's honorable service.

They lived in harmony and had two lovely children. She supported her husband in his quests, expeditions, cared for the household and children, while also cultivating the magic of her caste.

One day, however, she was speechless when her husband told her he had found his biological mother. Seeing the deep-flames in his eyes, she said without hesitation, "I see you want it! We can even move tonight!"

They did - as they had agreed- packing everything they owned and setting off on a sea voyage to the shores of Liberty Bay. This is how their new story began, built within the walls of the Order of the Argent Gryphon – Mayleea fulfilled herself there as a mother and supported the Order with the magic force of of nature.

Education
Blademaster.
Culture
Thaian.
Personality
Zealous, wrathful and callous.
Personal coat-of-arms
Per pale Azure and Or, bearing a rose Gules.
Faith
Banorism.

Defining traits:

Twin 
This character was born a twin.
Faithful Blade
Burning with a devout fervor, this character will not rest until the world is cleansed of all Zathrothites.
Loyal Retainer
Lacking any personal estate, this character lives by the favor of patrons through steadfast service.
Familial Kinslayer
This character is known to have slain a family member before a formal trial took place.

Childhood

Born to a Blademaster of the Guardian Nomads, the twins Sidney and Myrwen were raised within the safety of Wolftower’s walls. Their upbringing was shaped by duty and religious doctrine, and they sometimes accompanied their father on his rangings to learn how to track and kill wild warriors who preyed on travelers of the roads.

One fateful day, the three of them got captured in an ambush by a coven of witches in the Green Claw Swamp. How the twins escaped the ordeal was a mystery, but they returned to the Wolftower without their father, forever changed. While Myrwen chose to study the natural arts, Sidney followed their late father’s path to knighthood.

The nomad king Lonewolf, their father’s liege, sent Myrwen to the druids of Carlin and kept Sidney as his personal squire. He offered no warmth or care, only discipline, and the young squire learned to harden in place of grieving. Outside battles and training yards, stories on Banor’s heroic deeds occupied most of Sidney’s time, who sought them for spiritual clarity rather than comfort.

A decade later, when the time came to earn the spurs of knighthood, Sidney chose to venture deep into the Green Claw Swamp to find the trail of the murderous witches. It led to an aged, lonely woman, commanding from atop a hill, hosting an army of amazons and valkyries. At the witch’s side stood Myrwen, excited to be reunited with her twin.

The witch made no move to harm the squire. Instead, she smiled and opened her arms in a warm welcome, ready to embrace her child who had come home at last.

Long ago, a handsome young man came to visit the witch at night. It left her yearning for more. For years the witch courted her lover, offering gifts of life, immortality and great fame. The handsome man readily took her gifts, but refused her hand in marriage. You are a creature of the dark, the handsome man said, I cannot.

The next time he came to visit would be his last.

It dawned on Sidney then, though this loathsome heretic had stained their honour, she was their mother after all. Was not blood thicker than water?

And so, the young squire decided, and the witch's welcoming embrace was met by the hard steel of the sanctified blade.

When the tear-streaked Myrwen saw Sidney’s burning eyes as the bloodied sword raised a second time, she turned and fled into the wild swamps.

With their mother’s severed head mounted on the wall of the Wolftower, Sidney knelt before the nomad king, shedding tears of joy to have completed the difficult test. The pleasure of being hailed as a knight in Banor’s service was exhilarating.

Time to follow Myrwen’s trail before it ran cold.

Occupation
Steward of the Order of the Argent Gryphon.
Age
Late 50s.
Faith
Banorite.
Duties
- Maintains supplies and quarters for Order members and guests.
- Handles requests for resources, provisions, or accommodations.
- Offers practical advice and guidance to new Initiates.

Background

A former Commander of the Guardian Nomads, Barnabus was appointed steward of the Argent Gryphon for his great organizational skills. Though no longer a fighter, he reveres the Order's warriors and mages and would do anything to make their lives easier.

Overview

Name: Seraphine Duskvale
Race: Human
Profession:Mage
Personality: Composed, eloquent, compassionate

Background

Seraphine Duskvale believes that words can shape the world as surely as magic. Raised among scholars, she devoted her life to the pursuit of arcane knowledge while traveling the kingdoms, where she became known for her calm judgment and remarkable gift for bringing people together. Whether speaking before nobles, comforting the uncertain, or settling disputes between rivals, she has an uncanny ability to earn trust and leave others feeling heard.

Though a gifted mage in her own right, Seraphine rarely seeks recognition through displays of power. She believes wisdom is measured not by the spells one commands, but by the understanding one inspires in others, and she carries herself with a quiet confidence that naturally draws people to her.

Occupation
Sentinel of the Order of the Argent Gryphon in Senja.
Age
Late 40s.
Faith
Unknown.
Duties
- Patrolling the shores of Senja.
- Guards the Order's provisions.
- Protects the lady Miramis Blackheart, Castellan of  Senja.

Background

Little is known of Kjartan's past, except for the harsh climate of the Ice Islands that breeds stoic men like him. Though he speaks little, his loyalty is beyond reproach. He is known to patrol through blizzards and darkness alike, ever watchful for raiders, quara, or anyone who might threaten the peaceful island. The reason he was chosen for this post is his strong connection to the village and its people.
